title: Sagenia macrophylla
intro: Sagenia macrophylla, also known as the Senegalese fern or Giant Sagenia, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in African medicine, offering various health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Sagenia macrophylla include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sagenia macrophylla is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in the form of capsules or extracts, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
